buildRelationalSnowman.name: Build the relational snowman
buildRelationalSnowman.description: Derive all sizes and positions from `size` so the snowman scales correctly.

buildRelationalSnowman.name: Build the relational snowman
buildRelationalSnowman.description: Derive every radius and y position from `size`.

whereToStart.question: Where should I start?
whereToStart.answer: Start with the base, because it's the only circle anchored to anything fixed (the bottom of the canvas). Once you know `baseY`, you can stack the body on top of it, and then the head on top of the body.

baseSits.question: How do I work out where the base sits?
baseSits.answer: The bottom edge of the base sits `size` above the ground (Y=100). The center of the base is one base-radius further up from its bottom edge.

stackBody.question: How do I stack the body on the base?
stackBody.answer: When two circles touch with one directly above the other, the distance between their centers equals the sum of their two radii.

head.question: What about the head?
head.answer: The head sits on the body in exactly the same way as the body sits on the base.

whyDeriveFromSize.question: Why must everything be derived from `size`?
whyDeriveFromSize.answer: If you hardcode a number, changing `size` will leave gaps or overlaps between the circles. Using formulas keeps everything in proportion.

circle.description: Draw a circle centered at (centerX, centerY) with the given radius and color
